qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: IP whitelist bypass via X-Forwarded-For any-match logic in whitelist/ip.go
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about the IP whitelist middleware (whitelist/ip.go IsAuthorized) using any-match logic on X-Forwarded-For headers instead of checking only the rightmost trusted proxy IP. The advisory is about the ForwardAuth middleware failing to strip X-Forwarded-Prefix when trustForwardHeader=false. These are different middleware components, different headers (X-Forwarded-For vs X-Forwarded-Prefix), different vulnerable functions (IsAuthorized vs ForwardAuth subrequest builder), and different root causes (any-match iteration vs failure to rebuild a specific header).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: REST provider accepts unauthenticated PUT requests to inject arbitrary Traefik configuration
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding is about the REST provider (provider/rest/rest.go) accepting unauthenticated PUT requests to inject routing configuration. The advisory is about the ForwardAuth middleware failing to strip X-Forwarded-Prefix. These are completely different components, different code paths, different vulnerability classes (missing authentication on config API vs insufficient header verification in auth middleware), and different root causes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes a vulnerability in Traefik's ForwardAuth middleware where trustForwardHeader=false correctly rebuilds X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Host, and X-Forwarded-Proto but fails to strip or rebuild X-Forwarded-Prefix, allowing an attacker to spoof this header and bypass authentication. Neither qualifying finding identifies this root cause. The 'ip-whitelist-xff-bypass' finding describes a different vulnerability in a different middleware (IP whitelist vs ForwardAuth) involving a different header (X-Forwarded-For vs X-Forwarded-Prefix) with a different root cause (any-match iteration vs failure to rebuild header). The 'rest-api-config-injection' finding describes a missing authentication issue in the REST provider, which is a completely different component and vulnerability class.
evidence: Advisory root cause: ForwardAuth middleware's subrequest builder fails to strip/rebuild X-Forwarded-Prefix when trustForwardHeader=false (CWE-345).

ip-whitelist-xff-bypass references whitelist/ip.go IsAuthorized — a different middleware from ForwardAuth, handling X-Forwarded-For (not X-Forwarded-Prefix), with a different root cause (any-match logic vs missing header rebuild).
rest-api-config-injection references provider/rest/rest.go — the REST provider configuration endpoint, which is unrelated to ForwardAuth or X-Forwarded-Prefix header handling.
